program_en: B.Sc. in Aerospace Science and Earth Informatics & X Double Major Programme
program_chi: 航天科學與地球信息學及X理學士雙主修課程
learning_outcomes:
Aerospace Science and Earth Informatics & X Double Major Programme 1. Major Programme: Upon completion of the
Programme, students will have:
Knowledge
outcomes Interdisciplinary
knowledge By completing the programme,
graduates will have acquired knowledge of: (1) various theories, concepts and methods of aerospace science and Earth
system, mechanical engineering and automation, energy and environment,
geography and earth information science, and their applications; (2) the Earth and its environment, including their inter-dependent nature
for developing an in-depth understanding of their relationships with space
exploration and navigation; and (3) aerospace industry in the Greater Bay Area and beyond. Analyzing/Application
Ability By completing the programme,
graduates will have developed the following abilities: (1) a
life-long ability to analyze, explain and reflect on real-world earth
information, spatial development and resource management issues covered in
the curriculum; and (2) a
capacity to apply various theories and methods of earth information science and
resource management in their further studies and career development. Skills Outcomes Generic
Competencies By completing the programme,
graduates will demonstrate competency in: (1) integrating
knowledge in science and engineering, and solving interdisciplinary problems
through quantitative and qualitative data analysis; (2) effective communication, management, teamwork and leadership; (3) ethical values and responsibilities as professional member of local
and world communities; and (4) motivation in professional development, and self-managed and life-long
learning. Professional Skills By completing the programme,
graduates will have acquired professional skills on: (1) identifying
key issues related to aerospace science and the Earth system; (2) applying knowledge of engineering sciences and mathematics to the
discipline of aerospace science, earth informatics, mechanical and automation
engineering, energy technologies and environments; (3) designing and conducting experiments, as well as analyzing and
interpreting data; communicating and conveying results of the analysis to
both the general public and professional practitioners; (4) generating and evaluating alternatives, developing practical solutions
and proposing strategies in a global environment; and (5) problem-solving in an inter-disciplinary context. Attitude/Value Outcomes By completing the
programme, graduates will have acquired a positive attitude on: (1) awareness of the importance of engaging in life-long
learning and staying abreast of contemporary issues; (2) appreciation of different aspects of knowledge in aerospace science
and technology; (3) the continuing development of aerospace fields and the Earth system; (4) embracing the perspective of sustainable development and becoming
sensitive to their responsibilities with reference to spatial development and
resource management issues; and (5) developing a capacity to compare, contrast and respect the social,
economic and environmental values of places at different geographical scales
and the significance of local and regional conditions in the context of
globalization.

equivalent/substitute courses for details.

Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  Students
enrolled in the Double Major Programme should normally spend four regular
terms at CUHK(SZ) during the four years of study, except otherwise
specified in 2. and 3. Below. It applies to all students, including those
who select a 2nd Major offered by CUHK.
2.  |  Students
may apply for the Co-operative Education (Co-op) Programme (5 units) in
Year 2 or above. Students joining the Co-op Programme will undertake a
full-time placement in two specific work terms, including one summer
session and one regular term (i.e., term 1 or term 2). They have to spend
at least three regular terms at CUHK(SZ) during the four years of study.
3.  |  Students
who join term-time exchange programme have to spend at least three
regular terms at CUHK(SZ) during the four years of study.
4.  |  Students
enrolled in the Double Major programme can take substitute/equivalent/alternative
courses either at CUHK or CUHK(SZ). The list of
substitute/equivalent/alternative courses is available at this link (https://admission.cuhk.edu.hk/aseidm/). Students should not take a course
and its substitute/equivalent/alternative course. Should they do so, only
one course will be counted towards the fulfilment of units requirements.
Grades of relevant substitute/equivalent/alternative courses taken at CUHK(SZ)
will be transferred to CUHK for the purpose of calculation of GPA, Major
GPA and credit units.
5.  |  Students
normally have to declare their 2nd Major by the end of their
first year of study.
6.  |  All
courses at 2000 and above level and courses labeled as # in the
requirements of the 1st Major Programme, as well as 3 units of
research component course offered by the 2nd Major, will be
included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
7.  |  Courses
taken to fulfil the requirements of the 1st Major cannot be
counted towards the fulfilment of requirements of the 2nd Major, and vice versa.
8.  |  Under
very special circumstances and subject to approval by the Major
programmes concerned, students may opt out of the Double Major Programme
and register for the 1st Major programme only, and they will
be required to take an additional 3 units of research component course
offered by the 2nd Major to fulfill the unit requirement of
the 1st Major which is 60 units. The 3 units of research
component will also be included in the calculation of Major GPA. The
decision to opt out is irrevocable. Students
who have been approved to opt out from the Double Major Programme should
still observe the requirements in 1. above. If the students have not yet
declared a 2nd Major at the time of opting out, they should
fulfil the requirements of one of the areas under the 1st Major and take the additional 3 units of research component offered by
the 2nd Major corresponding to the particular area.
[a]  |  Students taking Physics offered by CUHK as 2nd Major are recommended to take MATH1010.
[b]  |  Students
taking Physics offered by CUHK as 2nd Major should take
PHYS1111.
[c]  |  For
students taking Earth and Environmental Sciences as 2nd Major,
research component EESC4820 will be included in the calculation of Major
GPA for honours classification.

1-unit continuous assessment course COOP4001. For
students who have to take more than 1 unit of course in the work term, they
should provide strong justifications for each additional course for
approval by the Co-operative Education Programme Committee and the major
programme.

[a]  |  Students may apply
to study in the Co-operative Education Programme (Co-op@CUHK) (5 units) in
Year 2 or above. Students who study the Co-op programme will take COOP4001,
which is a 1-unit course to be taken in each of the Summer Session of Year
3 and Term 1 of Year 4. They will undertake a full-time placement in two
specific work terms, during which they are not allowed to register for any
other courses except the 1-unit continuous assessment course COOP4001. For students who have to take more than 1 unit of
course in the work term, they should provide strong justifications for each
additional course for approval by the Co-operative Education Programme
Committee and the major programme.
[b]  |  When students are
studying at CUHK(SZ), they can take substitute/equivalent/alternative
courses at CUHK(SZ) to fulfill the requirements of CUHK.
[c]  |  The recommended
course pattern of College GE courses varies according to student’s college
affiliation.
[d]  |  Approval has been
granted by the Registrar for students of the Aerospace Science and Earth
Informatics & X Double Major Programme to take 19 units of courses in
Term 1 and/or Term 2 of Year 1.
[e]  |  Students have to
take 3 units of courses from item 2(a)(iii) of the study scheme. These
courses may be offered in other terms.
[f]  |  If students have
taken a 3-unit UGE course at CUHK(SZ), the 3 units will be transferred to
CUHK. Currently all GE courses in CUHK(SZ) are 3-unit.
